user_message: model a Japanese tea room interior

spec.subject: a traditional Japanese tea room (washitsu) interior with tatami floor, shoji screens, and tokonoma alcove

framing.camera: eye-level wide, slight low angle at 1.0m height, positioned in far corner looking diagonally across room

lighting.time_of_day: soft midday diffused light through shoji screens
lighting.key: warm diffused light 5200K bleeding through shoji paper screens from camera-right, simulating indirect sunlight
lighting.fill: cool bounce 7000K from opposite shoji panel, one-third key intensity, fills tatami shadows gently
lighting.rim: subtle warm backlight 4800K from tokonoma area candle/lamp separating alcove scroll from wall

palette.dominant: warm straw and sand — natural tatami rush matting, aged cedar wood #C8A96E
palette.accent: deep charcoal ink and matte black lacquer #2A2420 on tea utensils and wooden pillar
palette.neutral: translucent warm white shoji paper #F0EBE0 diffusing ambient light

composition.foreground: tatami mat surface with visible woven rush texture, edge border stripe in dark fabric
composition.midground: low chabudai table with iron tetsubin teapot, chawan bowl, and bamboo whisk; floor cushion (zabuton) beside it
composition.background: tokonoma alcove with hanging kakejiku scroll, single ikebana flower arrangement on shelf; shoji screen wall
composition.hero: the iron tetsubin teapot on the chabudai table, steaming slightly, catching the warm key light

materials.notes: anisotropic roughness ~0.8, fine woven displacement texture, slightly yellowed with age, dark fabric border edges, each mat ~0.9m x 1.8m

materials.type: translucent washi paper in cedar lattice frame
materials.notes: SSS or transmission 0.6, very slight warm tint, cedar grid 2cm thick with wood grain, subtle irregularity in paper translucency
roughness 0.7, long vertical grain, slightly darkened at base, no lacquer — raw natural finish
roughness 0.85, dark charcoal near-black, subtle bumpy surface displacement, matte not specular
roughness 0.2, deep brown-black lacquer, faint reflections of teapot, slight scratching on surface edges
roughness 0.5–0.7 variation, earth tones #7A5C4A, uneven glaze pooling at base, fingerprint texture on clay body

density.scattered: minimal — sparse placement of tea utensils on table; single flower stem in tokonoma vase; no clutter, ma (negative space) is intentional
density.control: deliberate asymmetric placement following Japanese wabi-sabi aesthetic; no symmetrical grid; zabuton slightly off-center; scroll hung slightly left of alcove center

spec.scale_notes: Room is 4.5m x 4.5m, ceiling height 2.1m — standard 8-tatami tea room. Chabudai table 0.6m diameter, 0.3m tall. Shoji screens 1.8m tall x 0.9m wide per panel. Tokonoma alcove 1.0m wide x 2.0m tall x 0.5m deep. Camera at 1.0m height, 3.5m back from table.
